title: Resilient Project Launch
definition: A two-tiered project launch strategy that ensures sustainability at a $0 operational baseline, while allowing for optional enhancements to increase resilience and inclusivity.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Creating a basic project with free resources, but also having the option to add paid support to make it more powerful and accessible to everyone.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A launch strategy that separates essential infrastructure from optional enhancements like TOR network integration, allowing for philanthropic support without altering the core design.
difficulty_levels.expert: A unique approach to project sustainability and inclusivity by offering a functional, free-tier baseline with optional, value-added enhancements, attracting both volunteers and philanthropists.

extra_fields.insight: The importance of a resilient project launch strategy that balances essential infrastructure with optional enhancements for maximum impact.
